sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The player is at rows 32-35, cols 16-19 (green) and rows 32-32, cols 16-19 (white). That's a strange shape. The player seems to have moved UP but is still at cols 16-19. The RIGHT moves didn't work because the player was blocked by the G-colored target clone at (32,20)-(35,23).
Wait, I see the issue. The G-colored node at (32,20)-(35,23) is blocking the player from moving RIGHT. This is the "collected" target clone that appeared when I collected the left target.
Let me try a different approach. I need to go around this obstacle. I could:
1. Move DOWN to get below the G target clone, then RIGHT
2. Move UP to get above it, then RIGHT
But the player is at rows 32-35. The G target clone is at rows 32-35, cols 20-23. To go around it:
- DOWN would put the player at rows 36-39, cols 16-19 (below the clone)
- Then RIGHT would work
But wait, I also need to get to the top-right target at (24,44)-(27,47). Let me think about the most efficient path.
Actually, let me reconsider the game. The player seems to be carrying the collected targets (the G-colored clones). Maybe the player needs to place them somewhere? Or maybe the G-colored clones are obstacles.
Let me try moving DOWN first to clear the G clone, then navigate to the top-right target.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player blocked by G-colored clone at (32,20)-(35,23)
# Need to go around it
# Move DOWN, then RIGHT to navigate around

action(['DOWN', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
